blockers.description: smartsheet-python-sdk 4.0.0 WHEEL is broken upstream (ships only version.py); the module/API are intact in the sdist. Migration is a decision, not a code problem: use `--no-binary smartsheet-python-sdk` (sdist) now, OR stay pinned <4.0.0 and wait for Smartsheet 4.0.1.
blockers.workaround: Stay pinned <4.0.0 (current state) — production green on 3.9.0.

decisions.rationale: Correct regardless of root cause (avoids broken 4.0.0 wheel); reversible; zero billing-logic change

decisions.decision: v1.2 = COMPAT-ONLY SDK migration (chosen over comprehensive rework)
decisions.rationale: Preserve pipeline per CLAUDE.md; additive only

decisions.decision: PDF: per-day composition (each day = own section; repeating print-title = [report header + that day's date + column header]); stitch to one PDF per WR
decisions.rationale: Excel print-titles are fixed top rows and cannot vary per day; per-day composition is the only way to repeat the correct date+columns on every spill page

decisions.decision: PDF engine: Excel COM locally / LibreOffice headless on CI
decisions.rationale: Local Windows has Excel (no LibreOffice); CI ubuntu has no Excel; both honor the same openpyxl print settings

decisions.decision: Buddy Mavin = Abraham Hernandez's 06/07 WRs (relabeled demo name)
decisions.rationale: No real foreman named Buddy Mavin exists

uncommitted_files: poc/ (UNTRACKED - do NOT commit: poc/_tools is 30MB pymupdf; poc/out has binary PDFs with real billing data)
next_action: Pick a strand: (A) on boss approval of poc/out PDFs -> brainstorm->spec->plan PDF productionization; (B) decide Phase 08 migrate-now vs wait-for-4.0.1 then /gsd-plan-phase 08; or (C) push local commits to origin.
context_notes: Multi-strand session. PRODUCTION IS SAFE: hotfix merged (a5c3fc8); weekly pipeline recovered (Run2271 green on 3.9.0). Phase 08 research found the 4.0.0 crash is a broken WHEEL (packaging bug), NOT an API break -> migration is tiny and arguably should wait for upstream 4.0.1. Most of the session became a PDF print POC: validated per-day composition fixing (1) sloppy mid-day spills (each spill page now repeats day+date+column header, reads as continuation) and (2) clipped date headers (day/date row merged full-width, never clips). Deliverables = one PDF per WR (mirrors Excel generation) in poc/out/. Reusable builder: poc/build_wr_pdf.py (uses poc/_tools/pymupdf for stitch+render; Excel COM for convert). PDF work is PRE-SPEC POC validation; productionize (new phase) only after boss sign-off.
